claim: In the days following the Champlain Towers South collapse, the Israel Defense Forces' Home Front Command — a unit experienced in international disaster-response (Haiti 2010, Mexico 2017, Typhoon Haiyan) — sent a search-and-rescue delegation to Surfside at the formal request of the Israeli government conveyed by Consul General Maor Elbaz-Starinsky. Prime Minister Naftali Bennett spoke with Florida Governor Ron DeSantis to coordinate the assistance; Diaspora Affairs Minister Nachman Shai visited the site. The delegation worked alongside Miami-Dade Fire Rescue and other U.S. search teams through the recovery operation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — “Surfside condominium collapse” (international assistance section)
sources.quote: Israel Defense Forces' Home Front Command search and rescue team to assist in the rescue efforts

sources.label: Times of Israel — “Israel sends IDF team to Florida to assist with tower collapse rescue efforts”
Times of Israel — “IDF delegation begins work at Florida disaster site as death toll rises to 9”

Times of Israel — “Departing IDF rescue team at Florida condo collapse get medals, farewell cheers”
Fight Back Podcast / @jakeshieldspod, Instagram reel DRoJ9FFiSUv (Nov 28, 2025) — surfacing source

notes: Anchor y2021-surfside. The IDF Home Front Command's involvement in Surfside is documented across Israeli and U.S. press, including departure-ceremony coverage. The reel implies a suspicious role for the IDF team relative to American rescue workers; the documented public record describes coordinated joint operations alongside Miami-Dade Fire Rescue under the standard international-disaster-assistance pattern that the Home Front Command has performed at several prior catastrophes.
